circuit: NullProofV2 — MiMCSponge commitment + nullifier + 7-level Merkle tree, 14554 constraints

contributions.type: SHA-256 of empty string (0x e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb924... — well-known, pre-existing, verifiable by anyone)

trustModel: 2-party ceremony. Phase 1 (ptau14) = 1000s of independent Hermez contributors. Phase 2: if EITHER contributor 1 (sls_0x) OR the beacon preimage is unknown to the attacker, the setup is sound. The beacon is SHA-256 of empty string — universally known — so the real trust anchor is contributor 1's randomness combined with the Hermez ptau14.
honestCaveats: The beacon (SHA-256 of empty string) is publicly known — security comes from contributor 1's randomness + Hermez ptau14.
For full production trust: run an N-party ceremony where contributors are independent and cannot collude.
External audit still required before production settlement use.
